target.title: feat(queue): persist followup queues across gateway restarts
target.body:
## Problem

When the gateway process restarts (npm update, crash, systemd restart), any
messages that arrived while an agent turn was in progress are silently lost.
The followup queue lives only in process memory ã there is no recovery path.

This is particularly noticeable on channels that serialize messages (e.g.
Telegram in queue mode): a message received during an active turn is queued
for delivery, then gone if the gateway restarts before that turn completes.

## Solution

Add persistence for the `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES` global map in shared SQLite state
(`state/openclaw.sqlite`), matching AGENTS.md runtime queue policy.

### Core: `src/auto-reply/reply/queue/persist.ts`

- `persistFollowupQueues()` ã serializes all non-empty queue entries into
  `followup_queue_entries` on `openclaw.sqlite`. Respects `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`
  via the existing `resolveStateDir()` utility. Rows are deleted when all
  queues drain to empty.
- `restoreFollowupQueues()` ã reads persisted rows at module init and
  repopulates `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES` before any enqueue or drain fires.
- `PersistedFollowupRun` ã a `Pick<FollowupRun, ...>` type that explicitly
  excludes runtime-only, non-serializable fields: `abortSignal`,
  `currentTurnContext`, `deliveryCorrelations`, `queuedLifecycle`. These are
  transient and must not survive a restart.
- Persisted routing selectors include `originatingReplyToId` and
  `inputProvenance` so restored runs keep reply-target context.

### SQLite layer: `src/infra/followup-queue-sqlite.ts`

- `replaceFollowupQueueEntries`, `loadFollowupQueueEntries`, and helpers used
  by persist/restore and doctor migration.

### Doctor migration: `src/infra/state-migrations.ts`

- Migrates legacy `live-chat-followup-queues.json` sidecars into shared SQLite
  and removes the JSON file when import succeeds.

### Mutation hooks

`persistFollowupQueues()` is called after every state-changing operation:

| Site | File |
|---|---|
| `enqueueFollowupRun` ã after `items.push` | `enqueue.ts` |
| Drain `finally` block ã both empty-queue delete and reschedule branches | `drain.ts` |
| `clearFollowupQueue` ã after `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES.delete` | `state.ts` |
| `refreshQueuedFollowupSession` ã after the run-rewrite loop | `state.ts` |

### Tests

`persist.test.ts`, `queue.followup-restart-roundtrip.test.ts`, and drain restart
tests cover:
- Shared SQLite rows written when queue has items
- Rows cleared when all queues are empty
- Full round-trip: enqueue ã persist ã simulated restart ã restore ã verify prompt + routing fields
- Runtime-only `abortSignal` field not present after restore
- No-op when no persisted rows exist
- Malformed entries (non-array items, null key) are skipped
- Telegram-routed followup redelivers exactly once after restart

## Notes

- The `draining: false` invariant is enforced on restore ã any in-flight drain
  that was interrupted is restarted by the normal drain scheduler on next enqueue.
- `summarySources` (contains `FollowupRun` objects, not serializable) is reset to
  `[]` on restore. `summaryLines` (plain strings) is persisted and restored.
- Schema adds `followup_queue_entries` to `openclaw-state-schema.sql`.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Queued followup messages received during an active agent turn survive a gateway restart and redeliver exactly once. Head `9ca2acbea` stores queues in shared SQLite (`followup_queue_entries`) and migrates legacy JSON sidecars via doctor.
- Real environment tested: Local dev checkout on Linux (`jewseppi/openclaw`, branch `feat/queue-followup-persistence`), Node 24, real temp state dir via `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, real `openclaw.sqlite` I/O (no in-memory mocks for persist/restore).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```bash
  cd openclaw
  pnpm exec vitest run \
    src/auto-reply/reply/queue.followup-restart-roundtrip.test.ts \
    src/auto-reply/reply/queue/persist.test.ts
  ```
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output (live module + real filesystem):
  ```
  RUN  v4.1.7 /home/joseph/dev/openclaw

   Test Files  2 passed (2)
        Tests  18 passed (18)
   Duration  2.53s

  Covered behaviors on head 9ca2acbea:
  - Telegram-routed followup queued mid-turn ã persisted to shared SQLite ã simulated gateway restart ã restore ã exactly-once redelivery ã no replay after second restart
  - originatingReplyToId + inputProvenance round-trip through persist+restore
  - auth profile selectors preserved, secret-bearing config stripped
  - legacy live-chat-followup-queues.json migrated by doctor into followup_queue_entries
  ```
- Observed result after fix: After simulated restart, restored queue items retain prompt, Telegram routing, reply target, and input provenance; drain delivers the restored prompt once; post-drain persistence prevents replay on a second restart.
- What was not tested: Full live native Telegram desktop capture (Mantis run failed on shared QA infra). Contributor-side live Telegram E2E remains blocked by gateway/session serialization constraints documented in prior PR comments.

ÞÊ Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
